info.topictitle: A4.3.3 Role of Hyperparameter Tuning in Supervised Learning Algorithms (HL only)

markdown:
## Understanding Hyperparameters


<definition term="Hyperparameters">Parameters set **before training** that **cannot be learned** from data.</definition>

Their role is to control the **learning algorithm’s behaviour** and have a major impact on model performance.

<callout type="hint">Think of hyperparameters as the **“dials”** you set before training begins.</callout>

<callout type="warning">Confusing **hyperparameters** with **model parameters** (weights/biases learned during training).</callout>

<callout type="example">**K-Nearest Neighbors (K-NN):**

* **K (neighbors):** How many points to consider when classifying.

**Decision Trees:**

* **Maximum Depth:** Prevents trees from growing too deep.
* **Minimum Samples per Leaf:** Controls when a leaf node is created.

**Neural Networks:**

* **Learning Rate:** Step size for updating weights.
* **Hidden Layers/Neurons:** Controls network complexity.</callout>

<callout type="hint">Always link the hyperparameter to the **problem type** in examples.</callout>

<callout type="warning">Choosing K in K-NN too small (overfitting) or too large (underfitting).</callout>

## Why Hyperparameter Tuning Matters


1. **Improves Performance:** Boosts accuracy, precision, recall, F1 score.
2. **Controls Overfitting/Underfitting:** 
  1. **Overfitting:** Too complex (e.g., deep trees, too many layers).
  2. **Underfitting:** Too simple (e.g., shallow tree, small K).

<callout type="hint">Balance model **complexity**: not too simple, not too complex.</callout>

<callout type="warning">Assuming defaults are always **optimal**.</callout>

## Evaluation Metrics in Supervised Learning


1. **Accuracy:** Correct predictions ÷ all predictions.
2. **Precision:** True Positives ÷ Predicted Positives.
3. **Recall:** True Positives ÷ Actual Positives.
4. **F1 Score:** Harmonic mean of precision & recall (useful for imbalanced data).

<callout type="hint">Use **F1** when precision and recall matter more than raw accuracy.</callout>

<callout type="warning">Reporting only accuracy on imbalanced datasets.</callout>

## Techniques for Hyperparameter Tuning


1. **Grid Search:** 
  1. Exhaustively tests all parameter combinations.
  2. Thorough
  3. Very slow.
2. **Random Search:** 
  1. Randomly samples combinations.
  2. Faster, 
  3. May miss best option.
3. **Bayesian Optimization:** 
  1. Uses probability models to predict good hyper parameters.
  2. Efficient for complex models, 
  3. Harder to implement.

![Hyperparameter tuning vs model training](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/675b9b2615bf04c8b53259b2f4c36e7f9200157b-588x322.jpg)
